meta.description: Production-grade Excel function catalog for safe formula insertion by AI agents

functions.name: ABS
functions.description: Returns the absolute value of a number
gotchas: Returns #VALUE! if argument is non-numeric text

functions.name: ACCRINT
functions.signature: ACCRINT(issue, first_interest, settlement, rate, par, frequency, [basis], [calc_method])
functions.description: Returns accrued interest for a security that pays periodic interest

functions.name: ACCRINTM
functions.description: Returns accrued interest for a security that pays at maturity

functions.name: ACOS
functions.description: Returns the arccosine in radians
gotchas: Domain is [-1,1]; values outside return #NUM!
Result is in radians, not degrees

functions.name: ACOSH
functions.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine
gotchas: Argument must be ≥1 or returns #NUM!

functions.name: ACOT
functions.description: Returns the arccotangent in radians
gotchas: Result range is (0, π)

functions.name: ACOTH
functions.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic cotangent
gotchas: Absolute value of argument must be >1

functions.name: ADDRESS
functions.description: Creates a cell reference as text

functions.name: AGGREGATE
functions.description: Performs aggregate calculation with options to ignore errors/hidden rows
gotchas: function_num 1-19 maps to different aggregates
options parameter controls what to ignore (errors, hidden rows, nested aggregates)
Cannot use with 3D references

functions.name: AMORDEGRC
functions.signature: AMORDEGRC(cost, date_purchased, first_period, salvage, period, rate, [basis])
functions.description: Returns prorated linear depreciation (French accounting)

functions.name: AMORLINC
functions.signature: AMORLINC(cost, date_purchased, first_period, salvage, period, rate, [basis])
functions.description: Returns prorated linear depreciation per accounting period

functions.name: AND
functions.description: Returns TRUE if all arguments are TRUE
gotchas: Short-circuits: stops evaluating after first FALSE
Empty cells are ignored

functions.name: ARABIC
functions.description: Converts a Roman numeral text to Arabic number
gotchas: Returns #VALUE! for invalid Roman numerals
Empty string returns 0

functions.name: AREAS
functions.description: Returns the number of areas in a reference

functions.name: ARRAYTOTEXT
functions.description: Converts an array to a text string
gotchas: format: 0=concise (comma-separated), 1=strict (with braces)

functions.name: ASC
functions.description: Converts full-width (double-byte) to half-width characters
gotchas: Primarily for East Asian languages (Japanese, Korean, Chinese)

functions.name: ASIN
functions.description: Returns the arcsine in radians
gotchas: Domain is [-1,1]; values outside return #NUM!

functions.name: ASINH
functions.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic sine
gotchas: Accepts any real number

functions.name: ATAN
functions.description: Returns the arctangent in radians
gotchas: Result range is (-π/2, π/2)
Use ATAN2 for full four-quadrant results

functions.name: ATAN2
functions.description: Returns arctangent from x and y coordinates
gotchas: Argument order is (x, y), NOT (y, x) — opposite of most programming languages
Both arguments 0 returns #DIV/0!

functions.name: ATANH
functions.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent
gotchas: Argument must be between -1 and 1 exclusive

functions.name: AVERAGE
functions.description: Returns the arithmetic mean
gotchas: Ignores text and logical values in ranges
Empty cells are ignored but zero is counted
Division by count of numeric values only

functions.name: AVERAGEA
functions.description: Returns mean treating text as 0 and logical TRUE as 1
gotchas: Text values are counted as 0
TRUE=1, FALSE=0
Empty cells still ignored

functions.name: AVERAGEIF
functions.description: Averages cells meeting a single condition
gotchas: Criteria supports wildcards
Cells with no numeric value in average_range are ignored

functions.name: AVERAGEIFS
functions.description: Averages cells meeting multiple conditions
gotchas: average_range is the FIRST argument
Up to 127 criteria pairs

functions.name: BASE
functions.description: Converts a number to text in given base
gotchas: Radix must be 2–36
Number must be non-negative integer

functions.name: BESSELI
functions.description: Returns the modified Bessel function In(x)

functions.name: BESSELJ
functions.description: Returns the Bessel function Jn(x)

functions.name: BESSELK
functions.description: Returns the modified Bessel function Kn(x)
gotchas: x must be >0

functions.name: BESSELY
functions.description: Returns the Bessel function Yn(x)
gotchas: x must be >0

functions.name: BETA.DIST
functions.description: Returns the beta distribution
gotchas: x must be between A and B (default 0 and 1)

functions.name: BETA.INV
functions.description: Returns the inverse of the beta cumulative distribution
gotchas: Iterative — may not converge for extreme parameters

functions.name: BIN2DEC
functions.description: Converts binary to decimal
gotchas: Input is text; max 10 characters (10-bit signed)
Leftmost bit is sign bit: 1111111111 = -1

functions.name: BIN2HEX
functions.description: Converts binary to hexadecimal
gotchas: places pads with leading zeros

functions.name: BIN2OCT
functions.description: Converts binary to octal

functions.name: BINOM.DIST
functions.description: Returns binomial distribution probability
gotchas: cumulative=TRUE for CDF, FALSE for PMF

functions.name: BINOM.DIST.RANGE
functions.description: Returns probability of trial result between two bounds

functions.name: BINOM.INV
functions.description: Returns smallest k where cumulative binomial ≥ alpha

functions.name: BITAND
functions.description: Returns bitwise AND of two numbers
gotchas: Arguments must be non-negative integers ≤ 2^48-1

functions.name: BITLSHIFT
functions.description: Returns number shifted left by shift_amount bits

functions.name: BITOR
functions.description: Returns bitwise OR of two numbers

functions.name: BITRSHIFT
functions.description: Returns number shifted right by shift_amount bits

functions.name: BITXOR
functions.description: Returns bitwise XOR of two numbers

functions.name: BYCOL
functions.description: Applies a LAMBDA to each column of an array
gotchas: Returns a single-row result with one value per column
The LAMBDA receives each column as a single-column array

functions.name: BYROW
functions.description: Applies a LAMBDA to each row of an array
gotchas: Returns a single-column result with one value per row

functions.name: CEILING
functions.description: Rounds up to nearest multiple of significance
gotchas: DEPRECATED — use CEILING.MATH instead
Sign of significance must match sign of number or returns #NUM!

functions.name: CEILING.MATH
functions.description: Rounds up to nearest multiple of significance
gotchas: Replaces CEILING for negative numbers
Mode parameter changes rounding direction for negatives

functions.name: CEILING.PRECISE
functions.description: Rounds up regardless of sign of significance
gotchas: Always rounds away from zero for positive, toward zero for negative

functions.name: CELL
functions.description: Returns information about a cell's formatting, location, or contents
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every change
info_type: "address", "col", "color", "contents", "filename", "format", "parentheses", "prefix", "protect", "row", "type", "width"

functions.name: CHAR
functions.description: Returns the character for a code number
gotchas: Uses the character set of your OS
CHAR(10) = line feed (newline in cells)
CHAR(9) = tab

functions.name: CHISQ.DIST
functions.description: Returns the chi-squared distribution
gotchas: x must be ≥0

functions.name: CHISQ.DIST.RT
functions.description: Returns right-tail chi-squared probability

functions.name: CHISQ.INV
functions.description: Returns inverse of left-tailed chi-squared distribution

functions.name: CHISQ.INV.RT
functions.description: Returns inverse of right-tailed chi-squared distribution

functions.name: CHISQ.TEST
functions.description: Returns the chi-squared test for independence
gotchas: Returns the p-value, not the chi-squared statistic

functions.name: CHOOSE
functions.description: Returns value from list based on index position
gotchas: index_num must be between 1 and number of values
Up to 254 values

functions.name: CHOOSECOLS
functions.description: Returns specified columns from an array
gotchas: Negative numbers count from the right: -1 = last column
Can reorder columns

functions.name: CHOOSEROWS
functions.description: Returns specified rows from an array
gotchas: Negative numbers count from the bottom
Can reorder rows

functions.name: CLEAN
functions.description: Removes all non-printable characters (ASCII 0-31)
gotchas: Does NOT remove non-breaking space (char 160) — use SUBSTITUTE(CLEAN(A1),CHAR(160),"")
Useful for cleaning imported data

functions.name: CODE
functions.description: Returns the numeric code for the first character

functions.name: COLUMN
functions.description: Returns the column number of a reference
gotchas: If omitted, returns column of the formula cell

functions.name: COLUMNS
functions.description: Returns the number of columns in an array or reference

functions.name: COMBIN
functions.description: Returns the number of combinations (n choose k)
gotchas: Both arguments must be non-negative integers
number_chosen must be ≤ number

functions.name: COMBINA
functions.description: Returns combinations with repetitions
gotchas: Arguments truncated to integers

functions.name: COMPLEX
functions.description: Creates a complex number from real and imaginary parts
gotchas: suffix: "i" (default) or "j"

functions.name: CONCAT
functions.description: Concatenates text strings and ranges
gotchas: Replacement for CONCATENATE — accepts ranges
No delimiter; use TEXTJOIN for delimited joining

functions.name: CONCATENATE
functions.description: Concatenates text strings (legacy)
gotchas: DEPRECATED — use CONCAT or TEXTJOIN instead
Does NOT accept ranges — must list individual cells

functions.name: CONFIDENCE.NORM
functions.description: Returns confidence interval half-width using normal distribution
gotchas: Assumes population standard deviation is known
For unknown σ, use CONFIDENCE.T instead

functions.name: CONFIDENCE.T
functions.description: Returns confidence interval half-width using Student's t-distribution
gotchas: Better for small samples or unknown population σ
size must be ≥2

functions.name: CONVERT
functions.description: Converts between measurement units
gotchas: Supports weight, distance, time, pressure, force, energy, power, temperature, volume, area, speed, and more
Unit strings are case-sensitive: "m" (meter) vs "M" (nautical mile)
Prefix support: "km", "cm", "mm", etc.

functions.name: CORREL
functions.description: Returns the Pearson correlation coefficient between two datasets
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Returns r value between -1 and 1; does NOT imply causation
Arrays must have same length
Ignores pairs where either value is non-numeric
A correlation of 0.3 in a small sample may not be statistically significant — use T.TEST or check p-value

functions.name: COS
functions.description: Returns the cosine of an angle in radians
gotchas: Input must be in radians; use RADIANS() to convert degrees

functions.name: COSH
functions.description: Returns the hyperbolic cosine
gotchas: Large arguments may overflow to #NUM!

functions.name: COT
functions.description: Returns the cotangent of an angle in radians
gotchas: Returns #DIV/0! when angle is 0

functions.name: COTH
functions.description: Returns the hyperbolic cotangent
gotchas: Returns #DIV/0! when argument is 0

functions.name: COUNT
functions.description: Counts cells containing numbers
gotchas: Only counts numeric values; use COUNTA for non-blanks
Dates and times are counted (stored as numbers)
Logical TRUE/FALSE in cells are NOT counted by COUNT

functions.name: COUNTA
functions.description: Counts non-empty cells
gotchas: Counts everything except empty cells
Counts error values, text, numbers, logicals
A cell with "" (empty string formula) IS counted

functions.name: COUNTBLANK
functions.description: Counts empty cells in a range
gotchas: Cells with "" (empty string) ARE counted as blank
Only takes a single range argument

functions.name: COUNTIF
functions.description: Counts cells meeting a single condition
gotchas: Criteria supports wildcards (* and ?)
Use ~* and ~? to match literal asterisk/question mark

functions.name: COUNTIFS
functions.description: Counts cells meeting multiple conditions
gotchas: Up to 127 criteria pairs
All ranges must have same dimensions

functions.name: COUPDAYBS
functions.description: Returns days from beginning of coupon period to settlement

functions.name: COUPDAYS
functions.description: Returns the number of days in the coupon period containing settlement

functions.name: COUPDAYSNC
functions.description: Returns days from settlement to next coupon date

functions.name: COUPNCD
functions.description: Returns the next coupon date after settlement

functions.name: COUPNUM
functions.description: Returns the number of coupons between settlement and maturity

functions.name: COUPPCD
functions.description: Returns the previous coupon date before settlement

functions.name: COVARIANCE.P
functions.description: Returns population covariance
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N (population) — use only when data represents the entire population, not a sample
For sample data, use COVARIANCE.S which divides by N-1

functions.name: COVARIANCE.S
functions.description: Returns sample covariance
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N-1 (Bessel's correction) — use for sample data
Requires at least 2 data points

functions.name: CSC
functions.description: Returns the cosecant of an angle in radians
gotchas: Returns #DIV/0! at multiples of π

functions.name: CSCH
functions.description: Returns the hyperbolic cosecant
gotchas: Returns #DIV/0! when argument is 0

functions.name: CUMIPMT
functions.signature: CUMIPMT(rate, nper, pv, start_period, end_period, type)
functions.description: Returns cumulative interest paid between two periods
gotchas: rate is per period (annual rate / periods per year)
Returns negative value (cash outflow)
type: 0=end of period, 1=beginning

functions.name: CUMPRINC
functions.signature: CUMPRINC(rate, nper, pv, start_period, end_period, type)
functions.description: Returns cumulative principal paid between two periods
gotchas: Returns negative value (cash outflow)

functions.name: DATE
functions.description: Creates a date from year, month, day components
gotchas: Months >12 or days >31 roll over automatically: DATE(2024,13,1) → Jan 1 2025
Year 0–29 → 2000–2029; 30–99 → 1930–1999; use 4-digit years to be safe

functions.name: DATEDIF
functions.description: Returns difference between two dates in specified unit
gotchas: UNDOCUMENTED function — not in Excel's function wizard but fully supported
unit: "Y"=years, "M"=months, "D"=days, "YM"=months ignoring years, "YD"=days ignoring years, "MD"=days ignoring months and years
start_date must be ≤ end_date or returns #NUM!

functions.name: DATEVALUE
functions.description: Converts a date string to a serial number
gotchas: Depends on system locale for date format interpretation
Returns only the date part; time portion is discarded

functions.name: DAVERAGE
functions.description: Averages values in a database column matching criteria
gotchas: database: range including headers
field: column name or index
criteria: range with header row and condition rows (OR logic between rows, AND within row)

functions.name: DAY
functions.description: Returns the day of the month (1-31)

functions.name: DAYS
functions.description: Returns the number of days between two dates
gotchas: Note: end_date is the FIRST argument (unlike DATEDIF)

functions.name: DAYS360
functions.description: Returns days between dates based on 360-day year
gotchas: method: FALSE=US/NASD (default), TRUE=European
Used in financial calculations

functions.name: DB
functions.description: Returns fixed-declining balance depreciation

functions.name: DCOUNT
functions.description: Counts cells containing numbers matching criteria

functions.name: DCOUNTA
functions.description: Counts non-blank cells matching criteria

functions.name: DDB
functions.description: Returns double-declining balance depreciation
gotchas: factor defaults to 2 (200%); set to 1.5 for 150% declining balance
May need to switch to straight-line in later years; VDB handles this automatically

functions.name: DEC2BIN
functions.description: Converts decimal to binary
gotchas: Range: -512 to 511
places pads with leading zeros

functions.name: DEC2HEX
functions.description: Converts decimal to hexadecimal

functions.name: DEC2OCT
functions.description: Converts decimal to octal

functions.name: DECIMAL
functions.description: Converts text representation in given base to decimal
gotchas: Radix must be 2–36

functions.name: DEGREES
functions.description: Converts radians to degrees

functions.name: DELTA
functions.description: Tests whether two values are equal (returns 1 or 0)
gotchas: number2 defaults to 0
Exact equality test — no tolerance for floating-point

functions.name: DEVSQ
functions.description: Returns the sum of squared deviations from the mean

functions.name: DGET
functions.description: Extracts a single value matching criteria
gotchas: Returns #NUM! if multiple records match
Returns #VALUE! if no records match

functions.name: DISC
functions.description: Returns the discount rate for a security

functions.name: DMAX
functions.description: Returns maximum value matching criteria

functions.name: DMIN
functions.description: Returns minimum value matching criteria

functions.name: DOLLAR
functions.description: Converts number to text with currency format
gotchas: Uses system locale for currency symbol
decimals defaults to 2

functions.name: DOLLARDE
functions.description: Converts dollar price from fraction to decimal
gotchas: fraction is the denominator (e.g., 8 for eighths)

functions.name: DOLLARFR
functions.description: Converts dollar price from decimal to fraction

functions.name: DPRODUCT
functions.description: Returns product of values matching criteria

functions.name: DROP
functions.description: Drops rows/columns from start or end of array
gotchas: Positive drops from start, negative from end

functions.name: DSTDEV
functions.description: Returns sample standard deviation of values matching criteria

functions.name: DSTDEVP
functions.description: Returns population standard deviation of values matching criteria

functions.name: DSUM
functions.description: Sums values in a database column matching criteria

functions.name: DURATION
functions.description: Returns the Macaulay duration of a bond
gotchas: Duration is in years
Higher coupon and yield → shorter duration

functions.name: DVAR
functions.description: Returns sample variance of values matching criteria

functions.name: DVARP
functions.description: Returns population variance of values matching criteria

functions.name: EDATE
functions.description: Returns date that is given months before/after start date
gotchas: Negative months go backward
End-of-month dates stay at end of month

functions.name: EFFECT
functions.description: Returns the effective annual interest rate
gotchas: Converts nominal (APR) to effective (APY) rate
npery = compounding periods per year

functions.name: ENCODEURL
functions.description: URL-encodes a text string
gotchas: Encodes spaces as %20, special characters as %XX

functions.name: EOMONTH
functions.description: Returns the last day of the month, N months away
gotchas: months=0 returns end of current month

functions.name: ERF
functions.description: Returns the error function integrated between bounds

functions.name: ERF.PRECISE
functions.description: Returns the error function ERF(x) from 0 to x

functions.name: ERFC
functions.description: Returns the complementary error function
gotchas: ERFC(x) = 1 - ERF(x)

functions.name: ERFC.PRECISE
functions.description: Returns the complementary error function (precise)

functions.name: ERROR.TYPE
functions.description: Returns a number corresponding to an error type
gotchas: 1=#NULL!, 2=#DIV/0!, 3=#VALUE!, 4=#REF!, 5=#NAME?, 6=#NUM!, 7=#N/A, 8=#GETTING_DATA
Returns #N/A if no error

functions.name: EVEN
functions.description: Rounds up to nearest even integer
gotchas: Rounds away from zero for negative numbers: EVEN(-3)→-4

functions.name: EXACT
functions.description: Compares two strings case-sensitively
gotchas: Returns TRUE/FALSE
Regular = operator is case-insensitive; EXACT is case-sensitive

functions.name: EXP
functions.description: Returns e raised to the power of number
gotchas: EXP(1) ≈ 2.71828; inverse of LN

functions.name: EXPAND
functions.description: Expands array to specified dimensions
gotchas: Pads with #N/A by default; use pad_with to change

functions.name: EXPON.DIST
functions.description: Returns the exponential distribution
gotchas: x must be ≥0, lambda must be >0

functions.name: F.DIST
functions.description: Returns the F probability distribution

functions.name: F.DIST.RT
functions.description: Returns right-tailed F distribution

functions.name: F.INV
functions.description: Returns inverse of left-tailed F distribution

functions.name: F.INV.RT
functions.description: Returns inverse of right-tailed F distribution

functions.name: F.TEST
functions.description: Returns the two-tailed p-value of an F-test for equal variances
gotchas: Returns p-value, not the F-statistic itself
Tests whether two samples have significantly different variances

functions.name: FACT
functions.description: Returns the factorial of a number
gotchas: Argument truncated to integer
Max is FACT(170); FACT(171) overflows to #NUM!

functions.name: FACTDOUBLE
functions.description: Returns the double factorial (n!!)

functions.name: FALSE
functions.description: Returns the logical value FALSE
gotchas: Parentheses optional: FALSE and FALSE() are equivalent

functions.name: FILTER
functions.description: Filters rows/columns based on Boolean criteria
gotchas: include must be same height as array for row filtering
Returns #CALC! if no matches and if_empty not specified
Can combine conditions: (cond1)*(cond2) for AND, (cond1)+(cond2) for OR

functions.name: FILTERXML
functions.description: Returns data from XML using an XPath expression
gotchas: Only available on Windows
Commonly paired with WEBSERVICE

functions.name: FIND
functions.description: Finds text within text (case-sensitive)
gotchas: Case-SENSITIVE; use SEARCH for case-insensitive
Returns #VALUE! if not found
Cannot use wildcards

functions.name: FINDB
functions.description: Finds text using byte positions (for DBCS languages)
gotchas: Same as FIND for single-byte character sets

functions.name: FISHER
functions.description: Returns the Fisher transformation
gotchas: x must be between -1 and 1 exclusive
Used to test significance of correlation coefficients

functions.name: FISHERINV
functions.description: Returns the inverse of the Fisher transformation

functions.name: FIXED
functions.description: Formats number as text with fixed decimal places
gotchas: no_commas: TRUE removes thousand separators

functions.name: FLOOR
functions.description: Rounds down toward zero to nearest multiple
gotchas: DEPRECATED — use FLOOR.MATH instead
Sign of significance must match sign of number

functions.name: FLOOR.MATH
functions.description: Rounds down to nearest multiple of significance
gotchas: Replaces FLOOR for negative numbers
Mode changes direction for negatives

functions.name: FLOOR.PRECISE
functions.description: Rounds down regardless of sign of significance

functions.name: FORECAST.LINEAR
functions.description: Predicts a y-value for a given x using linear regression
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Only fits a straight line — extrapolation beyond the data range is unreliable
known arrays must have same length and at least 2 points
Equivalent to INTERCEPT + SLOPE*x

functions.name: FORMULATEXT
functions.description: Returns a formula as a text string
gotchas: Returns #N/A if referenced cell has no formula

functions.name: FREQUENCY
functions.description: Returns a vertical array of frequency counts
gotchas: Returns array with one more element than bins_array
Must be entered as array formula in legacy Excel or spills in 365

functions.name: FV
functions.description: Returns the future value of an investment
gotchas: Cash-flow sign convention: payments out are negative
rate is per period; divide annual rate by periods
type: 0=end of period (default), 1=beginning
examples: =FV(0.06/12,120,-200) → future value of $200/month for 10 years at 6%
=FV(0.08,10,0,-10000) → growth of $10,000 lump sum at 8% for 10 years

functions.name: FVSCHEDULE
functions.description: Returns future value after applying a series of variable interest rates

functions.name: GAMMA
functions.description: Returns the gamma function value
gotchas: For positive integers: GAMMA(n) = (n-1)!
Returns #NUM! for non-positive integers

functions.name: GAMMA.DIST
functions.description: Returns the gamma distribution

functions.name: GAMMA.INV
functions.description: Returns inverse of the gamma cumulative distribution

functions.name: GAMMALN
functions.description: Returns the natural log of the gamma function
gotchas: x must be >0
More numerically stable than LN(GAMMA(x)) for large values

functions.name: GAMMALN.PRECISE
functions.description: Returns the natural log of the gamma function (more precise)
gotchas: x must be >0

functions.name: GAUSS
functions.description: Returns 0.5 less than the standard normal CDF
gotchas: GAUSS(z) = NORM.S.DIST(z,TRUE) - 0.5

functions.name: GCD
functions.description: Returns the greatest common divisor
gotchas: Arguments truncated to integers
Up to 255 arguments

functions.name: GEOMEAN
functions.description: Returns the geometric mean
gotchas: All values must be >0
Useful for growth rates and ratios

functions.name: GESTEP
functions.description: Returns 1 if number ≥ step, 0 otherwise
gotchas: step defaults to 0

functions.name: GROWTH
functions.description: Returns values along an exponential trend
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Fits exponential curve y=b*m^x — inappropriate for non-exponential data
known_y values must all be >0

functions.name: HARMEAN
functions.description: Returns the harmonic mean
gotchas: All values must be >0
Useful for averaging rates (e.g., speeds)

functions.name: HEX2BIN
functions.description: Converts hexadecimal to binary

functions.name: HEX2DEC
functions.description: Converts hexadecimal to decimal

functions.name: HEX2OCT
functions.description: Converts hexadecimal to octal

functions.name: HLOOKUP
functions.description: Searches first row and returns value from specified row
gotchas: DEPRECATED in favor of XLOOKUP
range_lookup: FALSE for exact match (almost always what you want)
Default is TRUE (approximate match) which requires sorted first row — a common source of bugs

functions.name: HOUR
functions.description: Returns the hour (0-23) from a time value

functions.name: HSTACK
functions.description: Horizontally stacks arrays
gotchas: Arrays with different row counts are padded with #N/A

functions.name: HYPERLINK
functions.description: Creates a clickable hyperlink
gotchas: link_location can be URL, file path, or cell reference (#Sheet1!A1)

functions.name: HYPGEOM.DIST
functions.description: Returns the hypergeometric distribution

functions.name: IF
functions.description: Returns one value if TRUE, another if FALSE
gotchas: Avoid deeply nested IFs (>3 levels) — use IFS, SWITCH, or XLOOKUP instead
If value_if_false omitted and test is FALSE, returns FALSE
If value_if_true omitted and test is TRUE, returns 0

functions.name: IFERROR
functions.description: Returns value_if_error if value is any error, otherwise returns value
gotchas: Catches ALL error types (#N/A, #VALUE!, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #NUM!, #NAME?, #NULL!)
Use IFNA if you only want to catch #N/A (more precise)
Can mask real errors — use sparingly and specifically

functions.name: IFNA
functions.description: Returns value_if_na only for #N/A errors; other errors pass through
gotchas: More precise than IFERROR — won't hide #VALUE!, #REF!, etc.
Preferred when wrapping VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP/MATCH

functions.name: IFS
functions.description: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns first TRUE result
gotchas: No default/else — add TRUE as last condition for a catch-all
Returns #N/A if no condition is TRUE and no catch-all
Up to 127 condition/value pairs

functions.name: IMABS
functions.description: Returns the absolute value (modulus) of a complex number

functions.name: IMAGINARY
functions.description: Returns the imaginary coefficient of a complex number

functions.name: IMARGUMENT
functions.description: Returns the argument θ (angle in radians) of a complex number

functions.name: IMCONJUGATE
functions.description: Returns the complex conjugate

functions.name: IMCOS
functions.description: Returns the cosine of a complex number

functions.name: IMDIV
functions.description: Divides two complex numbers

functions.name: IMEXP
functions.description: Returns the exponential of a complex number

functions.name: IMLN
functions.description: Returns the natural logarithm of a complex number

functions.name: IMLOG10
functions.description: Returns the base-10 logarithm of a complex number

functions.name: IMLOG2
functions.description: Returns the base-2 logarithm of a complex number

functions.name: IMPOWER
functions.description: Raises a complex number to a power

functions.name: IMPRODUCT
functions.description: Returns the product of complex numbers

functions.name: IMREAL
functions.description: Returns the real coefficient of a complex number

functions.name: IMSIN
functions.description: Returns the sine of a complex number

functions.name: IMSQRT
functions.description: Returns the square root of a complex number

functions.name: IMSUB
functions.description: Subtracts two complex numbers

functions.name: IMSUM
functions.description: Returns the sum of complex numbers

functions.name: IMTAN
functions.description: Returns the tangent of a complex number

functions.name: INDEX
functions.description: Returns value at the intersection of a row and column in a range
gotchas: row_num=0 returns entire column; column_num=0 returns entire row
INDEX+MATCH is the classic alternative to VLOOKUP
Can return a reference (useful in SUMPRODUCT, etc.)

functions.name: INDIRECT
functions.description: Returns the reference specified by a text string
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every change (performance impact)
Cannot reference other workbooks
Useful for dynamic sheet references
examples: =INDIRECT("A"&B1) → reference to cell A{value of B1}

functions.name: INFO
functions.description: Returns information about the current operating environment
gotchas: type_text: "directory", "numfile", "osversion", "recalc", "release", "system"

functions.name: INT
functions.description: Rounds down to the nearest integer
gotchas: For negative numbers, rounds AWAY from zero: INT(-3.2)→-4
Different from TRUNC which truncates toward zero: TRUNC(-3.2)→-3

functions.name: INTERCEPT
functions.description: Returns the y-intercept of the least-squares regression line
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Only for linear regression — check R² (RSQ) to verify linearity first

functions.name: INTRATE
functions.description: Returns the interest rate for a fully invested security

functions.name: IPMT
functions.description: Returns the interest payment for a specific period
gotchas: per must be between 1 and nper
Returns negative for loans (cash outflow)
IPMT + PPMT = PMT for any given period

functions.name: IRR
functions.description: Returns the internal rate of return for a series of cash flows
gotchas: Values must contain at least one positive and one negative
Returns rate per period; multiply by 12 for annual if monthly
May not converge — provide guess if needed
PRECISION GOTCHA: IRR assumes reinvestment at the IRR rate itself; use MIRR for different reinvestment rate assumption

functions.name: ISBLANK
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is empty

functions.name: ISERR
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is any error EXCEPT #N/A
gotchas: Use ISERROR to catch all errors including #N/A
Use ISNA for only #N/A

functions.name: ISERROR
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is any error
gotchas: Catches #N/A, #VALUE!, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #NUM!, #NAME?, #NULL!

functions.name: ISEVEN
functions.description: Returns TRUE if number is even
gotchas: Argument truncated to integer

functions.name: ISFORMULA
functions.description: Returns TRUE if the referenced cell contains a formula

functions.name: ISLOGICAL
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is TRUE or FALSE

functions.name: ISNA
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is the #N/A error specifically
gotchas: More precise than ISERROR — won't catch #VALUE!, #REF!, etc.

functions.name: ISNONTEXT
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is not text (including blank cells)

functions.name: ISNUMBER
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is a number

functions.name: ISODD
functions.description: Returns TRUE if number is odd

functions.name: ISOWEEKNUM
functions.description: Returns the ISO 8601 week number of the year
gotchas: Week 1 contains the first Thursday of the year
Different from WEEKNUM which may use Sunday as first day

functions.name: ISPMT
functions.description: Returns interest for straight-line declining loan (non-standard)

functions.name: ISREF
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is a reference

functions.name: ISTEXT
functions.description: Returns TRUE if value is text

functions.name: KURT
functions.description: Returns the kurtosis of a dataset
gotchas: Requires at least 4 data points
Returns excess kurtosis (normal distribution = 0)

functions.name: LAMBDA
functions.description: Creates a custom reusable function
gotchas: Last argument is always the calculation/body
Assign to a named range to create a reusable function
Can be used inline with MAP, REDUCE, SCAN, BYROW, BYCOL

functions.name: LARGE
functions.description: Returns the k-th largest value
gotchas: k=1 is equivalent to MAX
k must be between 1 and count of values

functions.name: LCM
functions.description: Returns the least common multiple
gotchas: Arguments truncated to integers

functions.name: LEFT
functions.description: Returns leftmost characters
gotchas: Default num_chars is 1
Returns empty string if num_chars is 0

functions.name: LEFTB
functions.description: Returns leftmost bytes (for DBCS languages)

functions.name: LEN
functions.description: Returns the number of characters in a text string
gotchas: Counts all characters including spaces
Does NOT count formatting

functions.name: LENB
functions.description: Returns number of bytes (for DBCS languages)

functions.name: LET
functions.description: Assigns names to values within a formula
gotchas: Improves readability and performance by avoiding repeated calculations
Names are scoped to the formula only
Last argument is the calculation that uses the named values

functions.name: LINEST
functions.description: Returns statistics for a least-squares linear fit
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Returns array {slope, intercept} or 5×N stats array when stats=TRUE
Statistics include standard errors, R², F-statistic, SS regression/residual
For multiple regression, known_x's can be multi-column
Must be entered as array formula in legacy Excel

functions.name: LN
functions.description: Returns the natural logarithm (base e)
gotchas: Argument must be >0 or returns #NUM!

functions.name: LOG
functions.description: Returns logarithm to a specified base
gotchas: Default base is 10 if omitted
Argument must be >0

functions.name: LOG10
functions.description: Returns the base-10 logarithm
gotchas: Argument must be >0

functions.name: LOGEST
functions.description: Returns statistics for an exponential least-squares fit
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Fits y=b*m^x; returns {m, b} or stats array
known_y values must all be >0

functions.name: LOGNORM.DIST
functions.description: Returns the lognormal distribution
gotchas: x must be >0; mean and standard_dev refer to ln(x)

functions.name: LOGNORM.INV
functions.description: Returns the inverse of the lognormal CDF

functions.name: LOOKUP
functions.description: Searches sorted vector and returns corresponding value
gotchas: DEPRECATED — use XLOOKUP or INDEX/MATCH instead
Data MUST be sorted ascending; unsorted data gives wrong results

functions.name: LOWER
functions.description: Converts text to lowercase

functions.name: MAKEARRAY
functions.description: Creates an array by applying LAMBDA to each position
gotchas: Lambda receives (row_index, col_index) starting from 1

functions.name: MAP
functions.description: Applies LAMBDA element-wise to one or more arrays
gotchas: Arrays must have same dimensions
Lambda receives corresponding elements from each array

functions.name: MATCH
functions.description: Returns the position of a value in a range
gotchas: match_type: 0=exact, 1=largest ≤ (sorted asc), -1=smallest ≥ (sorted desc)
Returns position (1-based), not the value itself
Pair with INDEX for powerful lookups

functions.name: MAX
functions.description: Returns the largest value
gotchas: Ignores text and logical values in ranges
Empty cells are ignored

functions.name: MAXA
functions.description: Returns the largest value including text (0) and logicals
gotchas: Text→0, TRUE→1, FALSE→0

functions.name: MAXIFS
functions.description: Returns the maximum value among cells meeting conditions
gotchas: Returns 0 if no cells meet criteria

functions.name: MDETERM
functions.description: Returns the matrix determinant
gotchas: Array must be square
Returns #VALUE! for non-square arrays

functions.name: MDURATION
functions.description: Returns the modified Macaulay duration
gotchas: Modified duration ≈ Macaulay duration / (1 + yield/frequency)
Measures price sensitivity to yield changes

functions.name: MEDIAN
functions.description: Returns the median (middle value)
gotchas: For even count, averages the two middle values
Ignores text and logical values in ranges

functions.name: MID
functions.description: Extracts characters from the middle of a text string
gotchas: start_num is 1-based

functions.name: MIDB
functions.description: Extracts bytes from middle of text (DBCS)

functions.name: MIN
functions.description: Returns the smallest value
gotchas: Ignores text and logical values in ranges

functions.name: MINA
functions.description: Returns the smallest value including text (0) and logicals
gotchas: Text→0, TRUE→1, FALSE→0

functions.name: MINIFS
functions.description: Returns minimum value among cells meeting conditions
gotchas: Returns 0 if no cells meet criteria

functions.name: MINUTE
functions.description: Returns the minutes (0-59) from a time value

functions.name: MINVERSE
functions.description: Returns the inverse matrix
gotchas: Array must be square and non-singular
Enter as array formula (Ctrl+Shift+Enter) in legacy Excel

functions.name: MIRR
functions.description: Returns the modified internal rate of return
gotchas: More realistic than IRR: uses separate finance and reinvestment rates
values must include at least one positive and one negative cash flow

functions.name: MMULT
functions.description: Returns the matrix product of two arrays
gotchas: Columns of array1 must equal rows of array2
Enter as array formula in legacy Excel

functions.name: MOD
functions.description: Returns the remainder after division
gotchas: Sign of result matches sign of divisor (unlike % in most languages)
MOD(n,0) returns #DIV/0!

functions.name: MODE.MULT
functions.description: Returns a vertical array of the most frequently occurring values
gotchas: Returns #N/A if no value repeats
Must be entered as array formula in legacy Excel

functions.name: MODE.SNGL
functions.description: Returns the most frequently occurring value
gotchas: Returns #N/A if no value repeats
If multiple modes, returns the first one encountered

functions.name: MONTH
functions.description: Returns the month (1-12) from a date

functions.name: MROUND
functions.description: Rounds to the nearest specified multiple
gotchas: Both arguments must have the same sign or returns #NUM!

functions.name: MULTINOMIAL
functions.description: Returns the multinomial of a set of numbers

functions.name: N
functions.description: Converts value to a number
gotchas: TRUE→1, FALSE→0, date→serial number, error→error, text→0

functions.name: NA
functions.description: Returns the #N/A error value
gotchas: Useful as a placeholder to indicate missing data
Cells with #N/A are skipped by chart series

functions.name: NEGBINOM.DIST
functions.description: Returns the negative binomial distribution

functions.name: NETWORKDAYS
functions.description: Returns working days between two dates (Mon-Fri)
gotchas: Includes both start and end dates in count
holidays is an optional range of dates to exclude

functions.name: NETWORKDAYS.INTL
functions.description: Returns working days with custom weekend definition
gotchas: weekend: string like "0000011" (Mon-Sun, 1=non-working) or number code
Allows defining non-standard work weeks

functions.name: NOMINAL
functions.description: Returns the nominal annual interest rate from effective rate

functions.name: NORM.DIST
functions.description: Returns the normal distribution
gotchas: cumulative=TRUE for CDF, FALSE for PDF
standard_dev must be >0

functions.name: NORM.INV
functions.description: Returns the inverse of the normal CDF
gotchas: probability must be between 0 and 1 exclusive

functions.name: NORM.S.DIST
functions.description: Returns the standard normal distribution (mean=0, σ=1)

functions.name: NORM.S.INV
functions.description: Returns the inverse of the standard normal CDF

functions.name: NOT
functions.description: Reverses the logical value

functions.name: NOW
functions.description: Returns the current date and time
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every worksheet change
Returns a serial number; format cell to display as desired

functions.name: NPER
functions.description: Returns the number of periods for an investment/loan
gotchas: Result may be fractional (partial periods)

functions.name: NPV
functions.description: Returns net present value of future cash flows
gotchas: CRITICAL GOTCHA: NPV assumes cash flows start at END of period 1, NOT at time 0
For initial investment at time 0: =NPV(rate,CF1:CFn)+CF0
This is the #1 most common NPV mistake in Excel

functions.name: NUMBERVALUE
functions.description: Converts text to number with locale-aware parsing
gotchas: Useful for parsing numbers from different locales

functions.name: OCT2BIN
functions.description: Converts octal to binary

functions.name: OCT2DEC
functions.description: Converts octal to decimal

functions.name: OCT2HEX
functions.description: Converts octal to hexadecimal

functions.name: ODD
functions.description: Rounds up to the nearest odd integer
gotchas: Rounds away from zero for negative numbers

functions.name: ODDFPRICE
functions.signature: ODDFPRICE(settlement, maturity, issue, first_coupon, rate, yld, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns price of a security with an odd first period

functions.name: ODDFYIELD
functions.signature: ODDFYIELD(settlement, maturity, issue, first_coupon, rate, pr, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns yield of a security with an odd first period

functions.name: ODDLPRICE
functions.signature: ODDLPRICE(settlement, maturity, last_interest, rate, yld, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns price of a security with an odd last period

functions.name: ODDLYIELD
functions.signature: ODDLYIELD(settlement, maturity, last_interest, rate, pr, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns yield of a security with an odd last period

functions.name: OFFSET
functions.description: Returns a reference offset from a starting cell
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every change (performance impact in large sheets)
Commonly used for dynamic ranges but INDIRECT or named ranges may be better

functions.name: OR
functions.description: Returns TRUE if any argument is TRUE
gotchas: Empty cells are ignored

functions.name: PDURATION
functions.description: Returns the number of periods for an investment to reach a target value
gotchas: rate must be >0

functions.name: PEARSON
functions.description: Returns the Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Mathematically identical to CORREL but may differ at ~15th decimal due to different internal algorithms
Both return r, not r²; square the result for coefficient of determination

functions.name: PERCENTILE.EXC
functions.description: Returns the k-th percentile using exclusive interpolation (0 < k < 1)
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: k must be strictly between 0 and 1 exclusive — k=0 or k=1 returns #NUM!
Uses interpolation method that excludes 0th and 100th percentile
Different from PERCENTILE.INC which allows k=0 and k=1
For small datasets, EXC and INC can give noticeably different results

functions.name: PERCENTILE.INC
functions.description: Returns the k-th percentile using inclusive interpolation (0 ≤ k ≤ 1)
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Uses inclusive interpolation — k=0 returns MIN, k=1 returns MAX
For large datasets, EXC and INC converge; for small datasets they diverge
PERCENTILE.INC(data,0.5) = MEDIAN(data)

functions.name: PERCENTRANK.EXC
functions.description: Returns rank as percentage (exclusive) in range (0,1)
gotchas: Returns #N/A if x is outside min/max of array

functions.name: PERCENTRANK.INC
functions.description: Returns rank as percentage (inclusive) in range [0,1]
gotchas: Min value returns 0, max returns 1

functions.name: PERMUT
functions.description: Returns the number of permutations

functions.name: PERMUTATIONA
functions.description: Returns permutations with repetitions

functions.name: PHI
functions.description: Returns the value of the standard normal density function

functions.name: PI
functions.description: Returns the value of π (3.14159265358979…)
gotchas: Takes no arguments; parentheses required

functions.name: PMT
functions.description: Returns the periodic payment for a loan or annuity
gotchas: Returns negative value for loan payments (cash outflow)
rate is per period — divide annual rate by 12 for monthly
IPMT+PPMT=PMT for any period

functions.name: POISSON.DIST
functions.description: Returns the Poisson distribution
gotchas: x must be non-negative integer, mean must be >0

functions.name: POWER
functions.description: Raises number to a power
gotchas: POWER(0,0) returns #NUM! in some versions
Equivalent to number^power operator

functions.name: PPMT
functions.description: Returns the principal payment for a specific period
gotchas: per must be between 1 and nper

functions.name: PRICE
functions.signature: PRICE(settlement, maturity, rate, yld, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns price per $100 face value of a coupon-paying security

functions.name: PRICEDISC
functions.description: Returns price per $100 face of a discounted security

functions.name: PRICEMAT
functions.description: Returns price per $100 face of a security paying interest at maturity

functions.name: PROB
functions.description: Returns probability that values fall between limits
gotchas: Probabilities must sum to 1

functions.name: PRODUCT
functions.description: Multiplies all arguments together
gotchas: Ignores text and logical values in ranges
Empty cells are ignored (treated as 1)

functions.name: PROPER
functions.description: Capitalizes the first letter of each word
gotchas: Also lowercases all other letters
Treats any non-letter as a word separator: O'BRIAN → O'Brian

functions.name: PV
functions.description: Returns the present value of future cash flows
gotchas: Cash-flow sign convention: result is negative if payments are positive
examples: =PV(0.08,10,0,-10000) → present value of $10K received in 10 years
=PV(0.06/12,360,-1500) → how much you can borrow for $1500/month

functions.name: QUARTILE.EXC
functions.description: Returns the quartile using exclusive interpolation
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: quart 1-3 only (not 0 or 4) — returns #NUM! for 0 and 4
Uses same interpolation as PERCENTILE.EXC

functions.name: QUARTILE.INC
functions.description: Returns the quartile using inclusive interpolation
gotchas: quart: 0=min, 1=Q1, 2=median, 3=Q3, 4=max

functions.name: QUOTIENT
functions.description: Returns the integer portion of a division
gotchas: Truncates toward zero (like TRUNC, not INT)

functions.name: RADIANS
functions.description: Converts degrees to radians

functions.name: RAND
functions.description: Returns a random number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive)
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every worksheet change
Not cryptographically secure

functions.name: RANDARRAY
functions.description: Returns an array of random numbers
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every change
whole_number: TRUE for integers, FALSE for decimals (default)

functions.name: RANDBETWEEN
functions.description: Returns a random integer between bottom and top inclusive
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates on every change
Both bounds are inclusive

functions.name: RANK.AVG
functions.description: Returns the rank of a number (average for ties)
gotchas: order: 0 or omitted = descending; 1 = ascending
Ties get the average of their positions

functions.name: RANK.EQ
functions.description: Returns the rank of a number (top rank for ties)
gotchas: Ties all receive the same (highest) rank
Next rank is skipped: ranks 1,1,3 not 1,1,2

functions.name: RATE
functions.description: Returns the interest rate per period
gotchas: Iterative — may not converge; provide guess if needed
Multiply result by number of periods per year for annual rate

functions.name: RECEIVED
functions.description: Returns amount received at maturity for a fully invested security

functions.name: REDUCE
functions.description: Reduces an array to a single value by applying LAMBDA
gotchas: Lambda receives (accumulator, current_value)
initial_value defaults to 0 if omitted

functions.name: REPLACE
functions.description: Replaces characters by position
gotchas: Position-based; use SUBSTITUTE for pattern-based replacement
start_num is 1-based

functions.name: REPLACEB
functions.description: Replaces bytes by position (DBCS)

functions.name: REPT
functions.description: Repeats text a given number of times
gotchas: Result limited to 32,767 characters

functions.name: RIGHT
functions.description: Returns rightmost characters
gotchas: Default num_chars is 1

functions.name: RIGHTB
functions.description: Returns rightmost bytes (DBCS)

functions.name: ROMAN
functions.description: Converts an Arabic numeral to Roman numeral text
gotchas: Number must be 0–3999

functions.name: ROUND
functions.description: Rounds to a specified number of digits
gotchas: Rounds 0.5 AWAY from zero (banker's rounding NOT used)
Negative num_digits rounds to left of decimal: ROUND(1234,-2)→1200
Floating-point: ROUND(2.15,1) may give 2.1 due to IEEE 754 representation

functions.name: ROUNDDOWN
functions.description: Rounds toward zero
gotchas: Always truncates; equivalent to TRUNC for positive numbers
ROUNDDOWN(2.999,0) → 2

functions.name: ROUNDUP
functions.description: Rounds away from zero
gotchas: Always rounds away from zero regardless of sign

functions.name: ROW
functions.description: Returns the row number of a reference
gotchas: If omitted, returns row of the formula cell

functions.name: ROWS
functions.description: Returns the number of rows in an array or reference

functions.name: RRI
functions.description: Returns equivalent interest rate for growth of an investment

functions.name: RSQ
functions.description: Returns the R-squared (coefficient of determination) for linear regression
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: RSQ = PEARSON(…)² — values near 1 indicate good linear fit
RSQ of 0.8 means 80% of variance in y is explained by x
Does NOT prove causation; always plot the data to check for non-linear patterns

functions.name: SCAN
functions.description: Like REDUCE but returns all intermediate results as an array
gotchas: Returns running/cumulative results

functions.name: SEARCH
functions.description: Finds text within text (case-insensitive)
gotchas: Case-INSENSITIVE; use FIND for case-sensitive
Supports wildcards: * (any chars) and ? (single char)
Use ~* and ~? to match literal * and ?

functions.name: SEARCHB
functions.description: Searches using byte positions (DBCS)

functions.name: SEC
functions.description: Returns the secant of an angle in radians
gotchas: Returns #DIV/0! at odd multiples of π/2

functions.name: SECH
functions.description: Returns the hyperbolic secant

functions.name: SECOND
functions.description: Returns the seconds (0-59) from a time value

functions.name: SEQUENCE
functions.description: Generates a sequence of numbers in an array
gotchas: Defaults: columns=1, start=1, step=1
Negative step for descending sequences

functions.name: SERIESSUM
functions.description: Returns the sum of a power series

functions.name: SHEET
functions.description: Returns the sheet number of a reference
gotchas: If omitted, returns sheet number of current sheet

functions.name: SHEETS
functions.description: Returns the number of sheets in a reference or workbook

functions.name: SIGN
functions.description: Returns the sign of a number: 1, 0, or -1

functions.name: SKEW
functions.description: Returns the skewness of a distribution
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Uses sample skewness formula (divides by (n-1)(n-2)/n) — requires ≥3 data points
Positive = right-tailed, negative = left-tailed

functions.name: SKEW.P
functions.description: Returns population skewness
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Uses population formula (divides by n) — different from SKEW which uses sample formula
Use when data represents the entire population

functions.name: SLN
functions.description: Returns straight-line depreciation per period

functions.name: SLOPE
functions.description: Returns the slope of the least-squares regression line
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Linear regression only — nonlinear data will give misleading slope
Equivalent to LINEST first coefficient

functions.name: SMALL
functions.description: Returns the k-th smallest value
gotchas: k=1 is equivalent to MIN

functions.name: SORT
functions.description: Sorts an array by a column or row
gotchas: sort_order: 1=ascending (default), -1=descending
by_col: FALSE=sort rows (default), TRUE=sort columns
For multiple sort keys, use SORTBY instead

functions.name: SORTBY
functions.description: Sorts an array by one or more external arrays
gotchas: by_array doesn't need to be in the original array
Supports multi-level sorting
examples: =SORTBY(A1:D100,C1:C100,-1,B1:B100,1) → sort by col C desc, then col B asc

functions.name: SQRT
functions.description: Returns the positive square root
gotchas: Negative argument returns #NUM!; use IMSQRT for complex results

functions.name: SQRTPI
functions.description: Returns the square root of (number × π)
gotchas: Argument must be ≥0

functions.name: STANDARDIZE
functions.description: Returns a z-score: (x - mean) / standard_dev

functions.name: STDEV.P
functions.description: Returns standard deviation of a population
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N (population formula) — use ONLY when data represents the ENTIRE population
For sample data, ALWAYS use STDEV.S which divides by N-1 (Bessel's correction)
The difference between STDEV.P and STDEV.S grows dramatically with small datasets: for n=5, STDEV.S is ~12% larger
Common mistake: using STDEV.P on survey/sample data underestimates true variability

functions.name: STDEV.S
functions.description: Returns standard deviation of a sample (the default you usually want)
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N-1 (Bessel's correction) — this is the correct choice for sample data
Use this unless your data is literally the entire population (e.g., all employees, all transactions ever)
Ignores text and logical values; use STDEVA if you need those counted
Requires at least 2 data points

functions.name: STEYX
functions.description: Returns standard error of the predicted y-value in linear regression
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Measures the scatter around the regression line — lower is better
Useful for constructing prediction intervals around FORECAST.LINEAR results

functions.name: SUBSTITUTE
functions.description: Replaces specific text with new text
gotchas: Case-sensitive replacement
If instance_num omitted, replaces ALL occurrences
instance_num specifies which occurrence to replace

functions.name: SUBTOTAL
functions.description: Performs subtotal calculation ignoring other SUBTOTALs in range
gotchas: function_num 1-11 include hidden rows; 101-111 exclude hidden rows
Ignores nested SUBTOTAL/AGGREGATE values
Commonly used in filtered tables

functions.name: SUM
functions.description: Adds all numbers in the arguments
gotchas: Ignores text and logical values within ranges
Direct logical arguments ARE counted: SUM(TRUE)→1
Up to 255 arguments

functions.name: SUMIF
functions.description: Sums cells that meet a single condition
gotchas: Criteria supports wildcards (* and ?)
If sum_range omitted, sums the range itself
sum_range is aligned by top-left cell, not full range shape

functions.name: SUMIFS
functions.description: Sums cells meeting multiple conditions
gotchas: Note: sum_range is the FIRST argument (unlike SUMIF)
Up to 127 criteria pairs
Criteria ranges must have same dimensions as sum_range

functions.name: SUMPRODUCT
functions.description: Returns sum of products of corresponding array elements
gotchas: Arrays must have same dimensions or returns #VALUE!
Powerful alternative to array formulas for conditional calculations
Treats TRUE as 1 and FALSE as 0 in Boolean arrays

functions.name: SUMSQ
functions.description: Returns the sum of squares of arguments

functions.name: SUMX2MY2
functions.description: Returns Σ(x²-y²) for corresponding elements

functions.name: SUMX2PY2
functions.description: Returns Σ(x²+y²) for corresponding elements

functions.name: SUMXMY2
functions.description: Returns Σ(x-y)² for corresponding elements

functions.name: SWITCH
functions.description: Matches expression against values and returns corresponding result
gotchas: More readable than nested IFs for exact-match scenarios
Last unpaired argument becomes the default
Up to 126 value/result pairs

functions.name: SYD
functions.description: Returns sum-of-years digits depreciation

functions.name: T
functions.description: Returns text if value is text, empty string otherwise
gotchas: Useful for ensuring a value is text
Numbers, dates, logicals return ""

functions.name: T.DIST
functions.description: Returns the Student's t-distribution

functions.name: T.DIST.2T
functions.description: Returns the two-tailed Student's t-distribution
gotchas: x must be ≥0

functions.name: T.DIST.RT
functions.description: Returns the right-tailed Student's t-distribution

functions.name: T.INV
functions.description: Returns the left-tailed inverse of Student's t-distribution

functions.name: T.INV.2T
functions.description: Returns the two-tailed inverse of Student's t-distribution
gotchas: probability is the two-tailed probability

functions.name: T.TEST
functions.description: Returns the p-value for a Student's t-test
gotchas: tails: 1 or 2
type: 1=paired, 2=equal variance, 3=unequal variance (Welch's)
PRECISION GOTCHA: Choose type carefully — type 2 assumes equal variances (use F.TEST to check); type 3 (Welch) is safer for unknown variance equality

functions.name: TAKE
functions.description: Takes rows/columns from start or end of array
gotchas: Positive takes from start, negative from end

functions.name: TAN
functions.description: Returns the tangent of an angle in radians
gotchas: Undefined at odd multiples of π/2 (returns very large number due to floating-point)

functions.name: TANH
functions.description: Returns the hyperbolic tangent

functions.name: TBILLEQ
functions.description: Returns bond-equivalent yield for a Treasury bill

functions.name: TBILLPRICE
functions.description: Returns price per $100 face value of a Treasury bill

functions.name: TBILLYIELD
functions.description: Returns the yield for a Treasury bill

functions.name: TEXT
functions.description: Formats a number as text with a specific format
gotchas: format_text uses Excel number format codes
Locale-dependent format codes for dates

functions.name: TEXTAFTER
functions.description: Returns text after a delimiter
gotchas: instance_num: negative searches from end
match_mode: 0=case-sensitive (default), 1=case-insensitive

functions.name: TEXTBEFORE
functions.description: Returns text before a delimiter

functions.name: TEXTJOIN
functions.description: Joins text with a delimiter
gotchas: ignore_empty: TRUE to skip empty cells
Accepts ranges unlike CONCATENATE
Max result 32,767 characters

functions.name: TEXTSPLIT
functions.description: Splits text into rows and/or columns
gotchas: col_delimiter splits into columns, row_delimiter into rows
Can use both for 2D splitting

functions.name: TIME
functions.description: Creates a time value from components
gotchas: Values >24h/60m/60s roll over
Returns a decimal between 0 and <1

functions.name: TIMEVALUE
functions.description: Converts a time string to a serial number (fraction of a day)

functions.name: TODAY
functions.description: Returns today's date (no time component)
gotchas: Volatile — recalculates daily
Returns serial number; format cell for desired display

functions.name: TRANSPOSE
functions.description: Transposes rows to columns and vice versa
gotchas: Must be array-entered in legacy Excel (Ctrl+Shift+Enter)
Spills automatically in Excel 365

functions.name: TREND
functions.description: Returns values along a linear trend
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Only fits linear model — use GROWTH for exponential data
Returns array; must be array-entered in legacy Excel

functions.name: TRIM
functions.description: Removes extra spaces (leading, trailing, and multiple internal spaces to single)
gotchas: Only removes ASCII space (char 32)
Does NOT remove non-breaking spaces (char 160); use SUBSTITUTE for those

functions.name: TRIMMEAN
functions.description: Returns the mean excluding a percentage of outliers from both tails
gotchas: percent is the total fraction to trim (split equally between tails)
TRIMMEAN(data,0.2) trims 10% from each tail

functions.name: TRUE
functions.description: Returns the logical value TRUE
gotchas: Parentheses optional

functions.name: TRUNC
functions.description: Truncates to a specified number of digits
gotchas: Truncates toward zero (unlike INT which rounds down)
Default num_digits is 0

functions.name: TYPE
functions.description: Returns the type of a value as a number
gotchas: 1=number, 2=text, 4=logical, 16=error, 64=array, 128=compound

functions.name: UNICHAR
functions.description: Returns the Unicode character for a code point

functions.name: UNICODE
functions.description: Returns the Unicode code point for the first character

functions.name: UNIQUE
functions.description: Returns unique values from a range
gotchas: by_col: FALSE=compare rows (default), TRUE=compare columns
exactly_once: FALSE=distinct (default), TRUE=values appearing exactly once

functions.name: UPPER
functions.description: Converts text to uppercase

functions.name: VALUE
functions.description: Converts a text representation of a number to a number
gotchas: Returns #VALUE! if text cannot be parsed as a number

functions.name: VALUETOTEXT
functions.description: Converts any value to text
gotchas: format: 0=concise (default), 1=strict (with quotes around text)

functions.name: VAR.P
functions.description: Returns variance of a population
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N (population formula) — use ONLY for entire population data
For sample data, ALWAYS use VAR.S which divides by N-1
VAR.P = STDEV.P² and VAR.S = STDEV.S²
The choice between .P and .S has major impact on confidence intervals and hypothesis tests

functions.name: VAR.S
functions.description: Returns variance of a sample (the default you usually want)
gotchas: PRECISION GOTCHA: Divides by N-1 (Bessel's correction) — correct for sample data
This is the standard choice for most analyses; use VAR.P only for full population data
Requires at least 2 data points
Ignores text and logical values

functions.name: VDB
functions.description: Returns variable declining balance depreciation (flexible)
gotchas: Automatically switches to straight-line unless no_switch=TRUE
Can calculate depreciation for partial periods

functions.name: VLOOKUP
functions.description: Searches first column and returns value from specified column
gotchas: range_lookup: FALSE for exact match (almost always what you want!)
Default TRUE (approximate) requires sorted data — most common VLOOKUP bug
Can only look right; use INDEX/MATCH or XLOOKUP to look left
Adding/removing columns breaks col_index_num — XLOOKUP is immune to this

functions.name: VSTACK
functions.description: Vertically stacks arrays
gotchas: Arrays with different column counts are padded with #N/A

functions.name: WEBSERVICE
functions.description: Returns data from a web service (GET request)
gotchas: Only available on Windows
Limited to 32,767 characters response
URL must return XML or plain text

functions.name: WEEKDAY
functions.description: Returns the day of the week as a number
gotchas: return_type changes numbering: 1=Sun(1)-Sat(7), 2=Mon(1)-Sun(7), 3=Mon(0)-Sun(6)
Default return_type=1 starts with Sunday=1

functions.name: WEEKNUM
functions.description: Returns the week number of the year
gotchas: Default: week containing Jan 1 is week 1, weeks start Sunday
Use ISOWEEKNUM for ISO 8601 standard

functions.name: WEIBULL.DIST
functions.description: Returns the Weibull distribution
gotchas: x must be ≥0, alpha and beta must be >0
Used in reliability and failure analysis

functions.name: WORKDAY
functions.description: Returns date that is N working days from start
gotchas: Skips weekends (Sat-Sun) and optional holidays
Negative days count backward

functions.name: WORKDAY.INTL
functions.description: Returns date N working days away with custom weekends
gotchas: weekend: string or number code defining non-working days

functions.name: WRAPCOLS
functions.description: Wraps a row/column vector into a 2D array by columns

functions.name: WRAPROWS
functions.description: Wraps a row/column vector into a 2D array by rows

functions.name: XIRR
functions.description: Returns IRR for cash flows on specific dates
gotchas: Dates must be in chronological order
Returns annualized rate regardless of cash flow spacing
More accurate than IRR for irregular cash flows

functions.name: XLOOKUP
functions.description: Searches a range and returns corresponding values
gotchas: Replaces VLOOKUP/HLOOKUP/INDEX+MATCH — more flexible and readable
Can look left, right, up, down
match_mode: 0=exact, -1=exact or next smaller, 1=exact or next larger, 2=wildcard
search_mode: 1=first-to-last, -1=last-to-first, 2=binary asc, -2=binary desc
Returns #N/A by default if not found; use if_not_found to customize

functions.name: XMATCH
functions.description: Returns position of a value (enhanced MATCH)
gotchas: match_mode: 0=exact, -1=exact or next smaller, 1=exact or next larger, 2=wildcard
search_mode: 1=first-to-last, -1=last-to-first, 2=binary asc, -2=binary desc
Replaces MATCH with more intuitive options

functions.name: XNPV
functions.description: Returns NPV for cash flows on specific dates
gotchas: rate is annual rate
First date is considered time 0 (unlike NPV which starts at period 1)
More accurate than NPV for irregular cash flow timing

functions.name: XOR
functions.description: Returns TRUE if an odd number of arguments are TRUE

functions.name: YEAR
functions.description: Returns the year from a date

functions.name: YEARFRAC
functions.description: Returns the fraction of a year between two dates
gotchas: basis: 0=US 30/360, 1=actual/actual, 2=actual/360, 3=actual/365, 4=European 30/360
Commonly used in financial calculations

functions.name: YIELD
functions.signature: YIELD(settlement, maturity, rate, pr, redemption, frequency, [basis])
functions.description: Returns the yield on a coupon-paying security

functions.name: YIELDDISC
functions.description: Returns annual yield for a discounted security

functions.name: YIELDMAT
functions.description: Returns annual yield of a security paying interest at maturity

functions.name: Z.TEST
functions.description: Returns the one-tailed p-value of a z-test
gotchas: If sigma omitted, uses sample standard deviation
Tests whether sample mean differs significantly from x
